codeReview.summary: Pass. The commit only repartitions the theme catalog data into smaller modules, keeps `themes.ts` as the single aggregation point, and preserves the full theme ID set and ordering needed for selection and persistence paths to keep working. I verified the old and new catalogs contain the same 52 theme IDs in the same order with no duplicates, and all touched theme files are under the 200-line mission limit.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `refactor-worker` skill requires `npm run test:integration` and repo-wide `npm run lint` in final validation, and says `followedProcedure` may be true only if all validation steps actually ran and passed. This feature handoff still reported `followedProcedure: true` after skipping `npm run test:integration` and replacing the full lint requirement with a scoped `npx biome check ./packages/extension/sidepanel/ui/settings/theme*.ts` because repo-wide lint was blocked by `history-list.ts`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/refactor-worker/SKILL.md:96-101,130 require `npm run test:integration`, `npm run lint`, and truthful `followedProcedure`; handoff `/Users/sero/.factory/missions/fdd24bf3-fc74-45b3-a646-4179615c1e31/handoffs/2026-03-11T13-51-30-029Z__sidepanel-refactor-themes__93151140-7690-4fca-a14c-0d93e0d3b350.json`:36-60 records only scoped Biome lint, notes the unrelated `history-list.ts` lint blocker, and still sets `followedProcedure` to true.

summary: Reviewed the worker transcript skeleton, handoff, skill instructions, mission shared-state files, and commit `065243a`. The refactor appears behavior-preserving: theme definitions were split across focused modules, `themes.ts` still assembles the catalog, the 52 theme IDs and ordering match the pre-refactor state exactly, and every touched theme file is below 200 lines. No code issues found; one skill/process shared-state observation was recorded about validation procedure reporting.
